title: Perturbation Theory of the Free Energy via the Mesoscopic Combined Partition Function
abstract: We develop a systematic perturbation theory for the Helmholtz free energy of a classical $N$-body system within the mesoscopic framework of~\cite{OsanoMeso,OsanoExtensivity}. The combined coarse-graining operator $\mathcal{C}=\mathcal{C}_x\circ\mathcal{C}_p$ acting on single-particle phase space partitions it into product cells $C_{i,α}=V_i\timesΠ_α$ and generates a mesoscopic partition function $\mathcal{Z}_{\rm meso}(λ)$ whose reference level factorises by the multinomial theorem: $\mathcal{Z}_{\rm meso}^{(0)}=(Z_1^{(0)})^N$. Perturbation theory for $\mathcal{F}_{\rm meso}(λ)=-k_BT\ln\mathcal{Z}_{\rm meso}(λ)$ in the inter-cell perturbation $\mathcal{V}_{\rm meso}$ yields the mesoscopic Gibbs--Bogoliubov inequality and an exact coupling-parameter integration formula. The full free energy satisfies \begin{equation*} F(λ)=\mathcal{F}_{\rm meso}(λ)-k_BT\!\sum_{i<j}I(i,j;λ)+O\!\left(|Λ|\ell^{-d}e^{-2\ell/ξ}\right), \end{equation*} where the inter-cell mutual informations $I(i,j;λ)$ are the corrections identified in the extensivity analysis. The first-order theory recovers the van der Waals equation and the Barker--Henderson result; the second-order term converges to the structure-factor formula in the fine-cell limit. For long-range interactions, factorisation fails, and the mutual-information corrections quantify the resulting non-extensivity.
summary: This paper presents a new way to calculate the free energy of classical systems by partitioning phase space into mesoscopic cells. It provides a mathematical bridge to correct for errors introduced by coarse-graining and shows how this framework recovers standard liquid state theories like van der Waals. It also identifies the WCA decomposition as the mathematically optimal way to split potentials in this framework.
main_problem: Developing a systematic perturbation theory for the Helmholtz free energy of a classical N-body system using a mesoscopic coarse-graining framework and quantifying the error introduced by cell-averaging.
main_result: The derivation of a connection formula relating the full free energy to the mesoscopic free energy via inter-cell mutual information corrections, and the proof that the WCA decomposition is the optimal split for minimizing mesoscopic variance.
method: A mesoscopic cumulant expansion of the free energy based on a combined coarse-graining operator in phase space, utilizing multinomial statistics and the linked-cluster theorem.
model_or_system: A classical N-body system of identical particles in a domain Lambda, described by a Hamiltonian consisting of a soluble reference part and a perturbative pair-potential.
key_observables: Helmholtz free energy, mesoscopic free energy, inter-cell mutual information, and reduced densities.
important_parameters: Cell diameter (l), correlation length (xi), particle density (rho), and temperature (T).
main_assumptions: Scale separation (xi << l << L) and the use of tempered potentials to ensure extensivity.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the mesoscopic framework, develops the perturbation theory via cumulant expansion, establishes the connection formula to the full free energy, analyzes the continuous limit, and discusses the optimality of the WCA decomposition.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
